bead.title: Story 16.3b: Restore GetBeadSnapshot p95 under 50ms latency budget
bead.description:
PROBLEM
The full-repo `cd cli && go test ./...` gate (parent AC 4) fails because `server.state.GetBeadSnapshot` has regressed past its latency budget: p95 measured at 72ms against a 50ms ceiling.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/graphql_bead_lookup_perf_test.go:290 `TestServerStateBeadSnapshotLatencyBudget` samples `fixture.server.state.GetBeadSnapshot` over the fixture bead set and at lines 309-311 fails when p95 > 50ms. Current p95 = 72ms, so the snapshot lookup path on server.state has regressed.

PROPOSED FIX
- Profile and restore `GetBeadSnapshot` (and its supporting index in internal/server/state) so the per-call p95 returns under the 50ms budget — e.g. ensure the warmed bead-lookup index is hit rather than rescanning, and avoid per-call allocations on the hot path.
- Do not relax the 50ms assertion; the budget is the contract.

NON-SCOPE
- Frontend e2e, spec-doc, and audit-event work (sibling/parent beads).
- The graphql efficacyRows and internal/agent timeout regressions (sibling beads 16.3c / 16.3d).
- New auth or role-based access control.
bead.acceptance:
1. cli/internal/server/graphql_bead_lookup_perf_test.go:290 `TestServerStateBeadSnapshotLatencyBudget` passes with GetBeadSnapshot p95 <= 50ms.
2. `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/...` is green.
3. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
